claim: Hands-on multi-model comparison against actual use cases beats benchmark-based evaluation, as benchmarks can be biased and contaminated with test data
actionable: Test models against your specific prompts and use cases using a notebook-based approach rather than relying only on published benchmarks
source_title: How good is llama 3.2 REALLY? Ollama SLM & LLM Prompt Ranking (Qwen, Phi, Gemini Flash)

claim: Llama 3.2 1B parameter model can perform comparably to larger models for many coding and text tasks despite being only 1.3GB on disk
actionable: Consider using Llama 3.2 1B (via Ollama) for on-device inference in resource-constrained environments where it matches performance of larger models
source_title: How good is llama 3.2 REALLY? Ollama SLM & LLM Prompt Ranking (Qwen, Phi, Gemini Flash)

claim: Qwen 2.5 7B parameter model performs exceptionally well on local device with 128k context window, rivaling state-of-the-art larger models
actionable: Test Qwen 2.5 (7B) via Ollama as a high-performing local alternative; it offers excellent quality despite smaller size
source_title: How good is llama 3.2 REALLY? Ollama SLM & LLM Prompt Ranking (Qwen, Phi, Gemini Flash)

claim: SLMs benefit from on-device privacy, offline access, content freedom, and personal data integration compared to cloud-based models
actionable: Evaluate SLMs for applications requiring data privacy or offline capability; local deployment eliminates cloud dependencies
source_title: How good is llama 3.2 REALLY? Ollama SLM & LLM Prompt Ranking (Qwen, Phi, Gemini Flash)
